rag_rules: Place input envelope assumptions in component type annexes using visible port names and explicit bounds from the requirement.
Use eq declarations to define boolean range conditions from numeric inputs, ensuring type consistency with AADL port types.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees must refer only to visible features or declared constants, never inferred or implicit values.
Contract clauses should mirror requirement bounds exactly—do not generalize or invent numeric limits.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` within component type or implementation scope.
Use `eq` to define intermediate boolean conditions for range checks, not direct boolean comparisons in guarantees.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Avoid converting Boolean or opaque ports into numeric range comparisons unless explicitly typed as real/int in AADL.
